speaker: RICHARD
text: Here pitch our tent, even here in Bosworth field.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My lord of Surrey, why look you so sad?

speaker: SURREY
text: My heart is ten times lighter than my looks.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My lord of Norfolk—

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Here, most gracious liege.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Norfolk, we must have knocks, ha, must we not?

speaker: NORFOLK
text: We must both give and take, my loving lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Up with my tent!—Here will I lie tonight.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But where tomorrow? Well, all’s one for that.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Who hath descried the number of the traitors?

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Six or seven thousand is their utmost power.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why, our battalia trebles that account.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Besides, the King’s name is a tower of strength

speaker: RICHARD
text: Which they upon the adverse faction want.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Up with the tent!—Come, noble gentlemen,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Let us survey the vantage of the ground.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Call for some men of sound direction;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Let’s lack no discipline, make no delay,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For, lords, tomorrow is a busy day.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: The weary sun hath made a golden set,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: And by the bright track of his fiery car

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Gives token of a goodly day tomorrow.—

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Sir William Brandon, you shall bear my standard.—

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Give me some ink and paper in my tent;

speaker: RICHMOND
text: I’ll draw the form and model of our battle,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Limit each leader to his several charge,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: And part in just proportion our small power.—

speaker: RICHMOND
text: My Lord of Oxford, you, Sir William Brandon,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: And you, Sir Walter Herbert, stay with me.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: The Earl of Pembroke keeps his regiment.—

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Good Captain Blunt, bear my goodnight to him,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: And by the second hour in the morning

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Desire the Earl to see me in my tent.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Yet one thing more, good captain, do for me.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Where is Lord Stanley quartered, do you know?

speaker: BLUNT
text: Unless I have mista’en his colors much,

speaker: BLUNT
text: Which well I am assured I have not done,

speaker: BLUNT
text: His regiment lies half a mile, at least,

speaker: BLUNT
text: South from the mighty power of the King.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: If without peril it be possible,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Sweet Blunt, make some good means to speak with him,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: And give him from me this most needful note.

speaker: BLUNT
text: Upon my life, my lord, I’ll undertake it,

speaker: BLUNT
text: And so God give you quiet rest tonight.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Good night, good Captain Blunt.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Come, gentlemen,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Let us consult upon tomorrow’s business.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Into my tent. The dew is raw and cold.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What is ’t o’clock?

speaker: CATESBY
text: It’s suppertime, my lord. It’s nine o’clock.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I will not sup tonight. Give me some ink and paper.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What, is my beaver easier than it was,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And all my armor laid into my tent?

speaker: CATESBY
text: It is, my liege, and all things are in readiness.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Good Norfolk, hie thee to thy charge.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Use careful watch. Choose trusty sentinels.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: I go, my lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Stir with the lark tomorrow, gentle Norfolk.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: I warrant you, my lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Catesby.

speaker: CATESBY
text: My lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Send out a pursuivant-at-arms

speaker: RICHARD
text: To Stanley’s regiment. Bid him bring his power

speaker: RICHARD
text: Before sunrising, lest his son George fall

speaker: RICHARD
text: Into the blind cave of eternal night.

speaker: RICHARD
text:
To
                 
                Soldiers
                .
                 
              Fill me a bowl of wine. Give me a watch.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Saddle white Surrey for the field tomorrow.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Look that my staves be sound and not too heavy.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ratcliffe.

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: My lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Sawst thou the melancholy Lord Northumberland?

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Thomas the Earl of Surrey and himself,

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Much about cockshut time, from troop to troop

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Went through the army cheering up the soldiers.

speaker: RICHARD
text: So, I am satisfied. Give me a bowl of wine.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I have not that alacrity of spirit

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nor cheer of mind that I was wont to have.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Set it down. Is ink and paper ready?

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: It is, my lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Bid my guard watch. Leave me.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ratcliffe, about the mid of night come to my tent

speaker: RICHARD
text: And help to arm me. Leave me, I say.

speaker: STANLEY
text: Fortune and victory sit on thy helm!

speaker: RICHMOND
text: All comfort that the dark night can afford

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Be to thy person, noble father-in-law.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Tell me, how fares our loving mother?

speaker: STANLEY
text: I, by attorney, bless thee from thy mother,

speaker: STANLEY
text: Who prays continually for Richmond’s good.

speaker: STANLEY
text: So much for that. The silent hours steal on,

speaker: STANLEY
text: And flaky darkness breaks within the east.

speaker: STANLEY
text: In brief, for so the season bids us be,

speaker: STANLEY
text: Prepare thy battle early in the morning,

speaker: STANLEY
text: And put thy fortune to the arbitrament

speaker: STANLEY
text: Of bloody strokes and mortal-staring war.

speaker: STANLEY
text: I, as I may—that which I would I cannot—

speaker: STANLEY
text: With best advantage will deceive the time

speaker: STANLEY
text: And aid thee in this doubtful shock of arms.

speaker: STANLEY
text: But on thy side I may not be too forward,

speaker: STANLEY
text: Lest, being seen, thy brother, tender George,

speaker: STANLEY
text: Be executed in his father’s sight.

speaker: STANLEY
text: Farewell. The leisure and the fearful time

speaker: STANLEY
text: Cuts off the ceremonious vows of love

speaker: STANLEY
text: And ample interchange of sweet discourse,

speaker: STANLEY
text: Which so-long-sundered friends should dwell upon.

speaker: STANLEY
text: God give us leisure for these rites of love!

speaker: STANLEY
text: Once more, adieu. Be valiant and speed well.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Good lords, conduct him to his regiment.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: I’ll strive with troubled thoughts to take a nap,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Lest leaden slumber peise me down tomorrow

speaker: RICHMOND
text: When I should mount with wings of victory.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Once more, good night, kind lords and gentlemen.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: O Thou, whose captain I account myself,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Look on my forces with a gracious eye.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Put in their hands Thy bruising irons of wrath,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: That they may crush down with a heavy fall

speaker: RICHMOND
text: The usurping helmets of our adversaries.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Make us Thy ministers of chastisement,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: That we may praise Thee in the victory.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: To Thee I do commend my watchful soul,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Ere I let fall the windows of mine eyes.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Sleeping and waking, O, defend me still!

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              EDWARD
text: Let me sit heavy on thy soul tomorrow.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              EDWARD
text: Think how thou stabbed’st me in my prime of youth

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              EDWARD
text: At Tewkesbury. Despair therefore, and die!

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              EDWARD
text:
(
                To
                 
                Richmond
                .
                )
                 
              Be cheerful, Richmond, for the wrongèd souls

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              EDWARD
text: Of butchered princes fight in thy behalf.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              EDWARD
text: King Henry’s issue, Richmond, comforts thee.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              HENRY
text: When I was mortal, my anointed body

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              HENRY
text: By thee was punchèd full of deadly holes.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              HENRY
text: Think on the Tower and me. Despair and die!

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              HENRY
text: Harry the Sixth bids thee despair and die.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              HENRY
text:
(
                To
                 
                Richmond
                .
                )
                 
              Virtuous and holy, be thou conqueror.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              HENRY
text: Harry, that prophesied thou shouldst be king,

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              HENRY
text: Doth comfort thee in thy sleep. Live and flourish.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: Let me sit heavy in thy soul tomorrow,

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: I, that was washed to death with fulsome wine,

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: Poor Clarence, by thy guile betrayed to death.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: Tomorrow in the battle think on me,

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: And fall thy edgeless sword. Despair and die!

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text:
(
                To
                 
                Richmond
                .
                )
                 
              Thou offspring of the house of Lancaster,

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: The wrongèd heirs of York do pray for thee.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: Good angels guard thy battle. Live and flourish.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              RIVERS
text: Let me sit heavy in thy soul tomorrow,

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              RIVERS
text: Rivers, that died at Pomfret. Despair and die!

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              GREY
text: Think upon Grey, and let thy soul despair!

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              VAUGHAN
text: Think upon Vaughan, and with guilty fear

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              VAUGHAN
text: Let fall thy lance. Despair and die!

speaker: ALL
text: Awake, and think our wrongs in Richard’s bosom

speaker: ALL
text: Will conquer him. Awake, and win the day.

speaker:
GHOSTS
               
              OF
               
              PRINCES
text: Dream on thy cousins smothered in the Tower.

speaker:
GHOSTS
               
              OF
               
              PRINCES
text: Let us be lead within thy bosom, Richard,

speaker:
GHOSTS
               
              OF
               
              PRINCES
text: And weigh thee down to ruin, shame, and death.

speaker:
GHOSTS
               
              OF
               
              PRINCES
text: Thy nephews’ souls bid thee despair and die.

speaker:
GHOSTS
               
              OF
               
              PRINCES
text:
(
                To
                 
                Richmond
                .
                )
                 
              Sleep, Richmond, sleep in peace and wake in joy.

speaker:
GHOSTS
               
              OF
               
              PRINCES
text: Good angels guard thee from the boar’s annoy.

speaker:
GHOSTS
               
              OF
               
              PRINCES
text: Live, and beget a happy race of kings.

speaker:
GHOSTS
               
              OF
               
              PRINCES
text: Edward’s unhappy sons do bid thee flourish.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              HASTINGS
text: Bloody and guilty, guiltily awake,

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              HASTINGS
text: And in a bloody battle end thy days.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              HASTINGS
text: Think on Lord Hastings. Despair and die!

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              HASTINGS
text:
(
                To
                 
                Richmond
                .
                )
                 
              Quiet, untroubled soul, awake, awake.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              HASTINGS
text: Arm, fight, and conquer for fair England’s sake.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              ANNE
text: Richard, thy wife, that wretched Anne thy wife,

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              ANNE
text: That never slept a quiet hour with thee,

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              ANNE
text: Now fills thy sleep with perturbations.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              ANNE
text: Tomorrow, in the battle, think on me,

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              ANNE
text: And fall thy edgeless sword. Despair and die!

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              ANNE
text:
(
                To
                 
                Richmond
                .
                )
                 
              Thou quiet soul, sleep thou a quiet sleep.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              ANNE
text: Dream of success and happy victory.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              ANNE
text: Thy adversary’s wife doth pray for thee.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              BUCKINGHAM
text: The first was I that helped thee to the crown;

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              BUCKINGHAM
text: The last was I that felt thy tyranny.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              BUCKINGHAM
text: O, in the battle think on Buckingham,

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              BUCKINGHAM
text: And die in terror of thy guiltiness.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              BUCKINGHAM
text: Dream on, dream on, of bloody deeds and death.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              BUCKINGHAM
text: Fainting, despair; despairing, yield thy breath.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              BUCKINGHAM
text:
(
                To
                 
                Richmond
                .
                )
                 
              I died for hope ere I could lend thee aid,

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              BUCKINGHAM
text: But cheer thy heart, and be thou not dismayed.

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              BUCKINGHAM
text: God and good angels fight on Richmond’s side,

speaker:
GHOST
               
              OF
               
              BUCKINGHAM
text: And Richard fall in height of all his pride.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Give me another horse! Bind up my wounds!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Have mercy, Jesu!—Soft, I did but dream.

speaker: RICHARD
text: O coward conscience, how dost thou afflict me!

speaker: RICHARD
text: The lights burn blue; it is now dead midnight.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Cold fearful drops stand on my trembling flesh.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What do I fear? Myself? There’s none else by.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Richard loves Richard, that is, I am I.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Is there a murderer here? No. Yes, I am.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then fly! What, from myself? Great reason why:

speaker: RICHARD
text: Lest I revenge. What, myself upon myself?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Alack, I love myself. Wherefore? For any good

speaker: RICHARD
text: That I myself have done unto myself?

speaker: RICHARD
text: O, no. Alas, I rather hate myself

speaker: RICHARD
text: For hateful deeds committed by myself.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I am a villain. Yet I lie; I am not.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Fool, of thyself speak well. Fool, do not flatter.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My conscience hath a thousand several tongues,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And every tongue brings in a several tale,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And every tale condemns me for a villain.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Perjury, perjury, in the highest degree;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Murder, stern murder, in the direst degree;

speaker: RICHARD
text: All several sins, all used in each degree,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Throng to the bar, crying all “Guilty, guilty!”

speaker: RICHARD
text: I shall despair. There is no creature loves me,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And if I die no soul will pity me.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And wherefore should they, since that I myself

speaker: RICHARD
text: Find in myself no pity to myself?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Methought the souls of all that I had murdered

speaker: RICHARD
text: Came to my tent, and every one did threat

speaker: RICHARD
text: Tomorrow’s vengeance on the head of Richard.

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: My lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Zounds, who is there?

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Ratcliffe, my lord, ’tis I. The early village cock

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Hath twice done salutation to the morn.

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Your friends are up and buckle on their armor.

speaker: RICHARD
text: O Ratcliffe, I have dreamed a fearful dream!

speaker: RICHARD
text: What think’st thou, will our friends prove all true?

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: No doubt, my lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: O Ratcliffe, I fear, I fear.

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Nay, good my lord, be not afraid of shadows.

speaker: RICHARD
text: By the apostle Paul, shadows tonight

speaker: RICHARD
text: Have struck more terror to the soul of Richard

speaker: RICHARD
text: Than can the substance of ten thousand soldiers

speaker: RICHARD
text: Armed in proof and led by shallow Richmond.

speaker: RICHARD
text: ’Tis not yet near day. Come, go with me.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Under our tents I’ll play the eavesdropper

speaker: RICHARD
text: To see if any mean to shrink from me.

speaker: LORDS
text: Good morrow, Richmond.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Cry mercy, lords and watchful gentlemen,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: That you have ta’en a tardy sluggard here.

speaker:
A
               
              LORD
text: How have you slept, my lord?

speaker: RICHMOND
text: The sweetest sleep and fairest-boding dreams

speaker: RICHMOND
text: That ever entered in a drowsy head

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Have I since your departure had, my lords.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Methought their souls whose bodies Richard murdered

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Came to my tent and cried on victory.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: I promise you, my soul is very jocund

speaker: RICHMOND
text: In the remembrance of so fair a dream.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: How far into the morning is it, lords?

speaker:
A
               
              LORD
text: Upon the stroke of four.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Why, then ’tis time to arm and give direction.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: More than I have said, loving countrymen,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: The leisure and enforcement of the time

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Forbids to dwell upon. Yet remember this:

speaker: RICHMOND
text: God, and our good cause, fight upon our side.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: The prayers of holy saints and wrongèd souls,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Like high-reared bulwarks, stand before our faces.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Richard except, those whom we fight against

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Had rather have us win than him they follow.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: For what is he they follow? Truly, gentlemen,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: A bloody tyrant and a homicide;

speaker: RICHMOND
text: One raised in blood, and one in blood established;

speaker: RICHMOND
text: One that made means to come by what he hath,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: And slaughtered those that were the means to help him;

speaker: RICHMOND
text: A base foul stone, made precious by the foil

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Of England’s chair, where he is falsely set;

speaker: RICHMOND
text: One that hath ever been God’s enemy.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Then if you fight against God’s enemy,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: God will, in justice, ward you as his soldiers.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: If you do sweat to put a tyrant down,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: You sleep in peace, the tyrant being slain.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: If you do fight against your country’s foes,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Your country’s fat shall pay your pains the hire.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: If you do fight in safeguard of your wives,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Your wives shall welcome home the conquerors.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: If you do free your children from the sword,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Your children’s children quits it in your age.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Then, in the name of God and all these rights,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Advance your standards; draw your willing swords.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: For me, the ransom of my bold attempt

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Shall be this cold corpse on the Earth’s cold face,

speaker: RICHMOND
text: But if I thrive, the gain of my attempt

speaker: RICHMOND
text: The least of you shall share his part thereof.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: Sound drums and trumpets boldly and cheerfully.

speaker: RICHMOND
text: God, and Saint George, Richmond, and victory!

speaker: RICHARD
text: What said Northumberland as touching Richmond?

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: That he was never trainèd up in arms.

speaker: RICHARD
text: He said the truth. And what said Surrey then?

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: He smiled and said “The better for our purpose.”

speaker: RICHARD
text: He was in the right, and so indeed it is.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Tell the clock there. Give me a calendar.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Who saw the sun today?

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: Not I, my lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then he disdains to shine, for by the book

speaker: RICHARD
text: He should have braved the east an hour ago.

speaker: RICHARD
text: A black day will it be to somebody.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ratcliffe!

speaker: RATCLIFFE
text: My lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The sun will not be seen today.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The sky doth frown and lour upon our army.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I would these dewy tears were from the ground.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Not shine today? Why, what is that to me

speaker: RICHARD
text: More than to Richmond, for the selfsame heaven

speaker: RICHARD
text: That frowns on me looks sadly upon him.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Arm, arm, my lord. The foe vaunts in the field.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Come, bustle, bustle. Caparison my horse.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Call up Lord Stanley; bid him bring his power.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: I will lead forth my soldiers to the plain,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And thus my battle shall be orderèd:

speaker: RICHARD
text: My foreward shall be drawn out all in length,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Consisting equally of horse and foot;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Our archers shall be placèd in the midst.

speaker: RICHARD
text: John Duke of Norfolk, Thomas Earl of Surrey,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Shall have the leading of this foot and horse.

speaker: RICHARD
text: They thus directed, we will follow

speaker: RICHARD
text: In the main battle, whose puissance on either side

speaker: RICHARD
text: Shall be well wingèd with our chiefest horse.

speaker: RICHARD
text: This, and Saint George to boot!—What think’st thou, Norfolk?

speaker: NORFOLK
text: A good direction, warlike sovereign.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: This found I on my tent this morning.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Jockey of Norfolk, be not so bold.

speaker: RICHARD
text: For Dickon thy master is bought and sold.

speaker: RICHARD
text: A thing devisèd by the enemy.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Go, gentlemen, every man unto his charge.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Let not our babbling dreams affright our souls.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Conscience is but a word that cowards use,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Devised at first to keep the strong in awe.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Our strong arms be our conscience, swords our law.

speaker: RICHARD
text: March on. Join bravely. Let us to it pell mell,

speaker: RICHARD
text: If not to heaven, then hand in hand to hell.

speaker: RICHARD
text: What shall I say more than I have inferred?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Remember whom you are to cope withal,

speaker: RICHARD
text: A sort of vagabonds, rascals, and runaways,

speaker: RICHARD
text: A scum of Bretons and base lackey peasants,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Whom their o’ercloyèd country vomits forth

speaker: RICHARD
text: To desperate adventures and assured destruction.

speaker: RICHARD
text: You sleeping safe, they bring to you unrest;

speaker: RICHARD
text: You having lands and blessed with beauteous wives,

speaker: RICHARD
text: They would restrain the one, distain the other.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And who doth lead them but a paltry fellow,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Long kept in Brittany at our mother’s cost,

speaker: RICHARD
text: A milksop, one that never in his life

speaker: RICHARD
text: Felt so much cold as overshoes in snow?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Let’s whip these stragglers o’er the seas again,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Lash hence these overweening rags of France,

speaker: RICHARD
text: These famished beggars weary of their lives,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Who, but for dreaming on this fond exploit,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For want of means, poor rats, had hanged themselves.

speaker: RICHARD
text: If we be conquered, let men conquer us,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And not these bastard Bretons, whom our fathers

speaker: RICHARD
text: Have in their own land beaten, bobbed, and thumped,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And in record left them the heirs of shame.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Shall these enjoy our lands, lie with our wives,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ravish our daughters?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Hark, I hear their drum.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Fight, gentlemen of England.—Fight, bold yeomen.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Draw, archers; draw your arrows to the head.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: Spur your proud horses hard, and ride in blood.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Amaze the welkin with your broken staves.—

speaker: RICHARD
text: What says Lord Stanley? Will he bring his power?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: My lord, he doth deny to come.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Off with his son George’s head!

speaker: NORFOLK
text: My lord, the enemy is past the marsh.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: After the battle let George Stanley die.

speaker: RICHARD
text: A thousand hearts are great within my bosom.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Advance our standards. Set upon our foes.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Our ancient word of courage, fair Saint George,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Inspire us with the spleen of fiery dragons.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Upon them! Victory sits on our helms.
